items.shape_or_design: thin elongated chain with simple links
items.rationale: Visible thin elongated gold-colored chain, typical weight for thin chains is 5-8g; assumed 22K for traditional Indian jewellery. Value includes conservative making charges.

items.shape_or_design: rounded linked bead-like elements forming a necklace
items.rationale: Beaded necklace visually heavier than thin chain, estimated 20-28g for medium size bead-linked necklace. Assumed 22K purity. Included conservative making charges.

items.shape_or_design: broad flat band with fine detailed segments
items.rationale: Broad flat necklace typically heavier, conservatively estimated 35-45g. Assumed 22K purity, traditional design likely high purity. Conservative making charges included.

items.type: delicate chain or necklace with small pendant
items.shape_or_design: thin chain with small beads or pendants attached
items.rationale: Smaller delicate chain with possible pearl or stone pendants; metal weight reduced due to stones, assumed 18K purity due to design complexity and stones. Overlap and occlusion limit precise weight.

items.shape_or_design: cylindrical stacked bangles with raised patterns
items.rationale: Stack of 3 bangles visually substantial; typical weight per bangle approx 15-18g; total adjusted to 45-55g. Traditional bangles usually high purity 22K assumed. Making charges considered.

items.type: pair of studs or small earrings
items.shape_or_design: two small round floral shapes with petals
items.rationale: Pair of small studs floral design, usually light weight, estimated 1.5-2.5g each. Assumed 22K purity. Conservative making charges applied.

assumptions: The gold rate used is INR 12,435 per gram for 22K and INR 10,174 per gram for 18K as provided from Goodreturns on 23 March 2026.
Making charges have been conservatively included by adding approximately 10-20% on metal value for each item.
Purity is estimated visually; traditional Indian gold jewelry is often 22K, stones or pendants reduce purity to likely 18K.
Weight estimates are bounded ranges assuming conservative underestimation to avoid overvaluation due to partial overlap and occlusion.
Each item corresponds directly to a Stage 1 item_id without merging similar items.
